options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: death_link
options.description:
When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.
Cart: DeathLinks are sent upon carting, and receiving will cart the player.
Quest: DeathLinks are sent upon failing a quest, and receiving will fail the current quest.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Village Low: Complete the Village Elder Urgent Quest "A State of Crisis!"
Village High: Complete the Nekoht Quest "Rise to the Summit"
Monster Hunter: Complete the Nekoht Quest "Monster Hunter"
Low Rank: Complete the Low->High Urgent Quest "The Approaching Gaoren"
High Rank: Complete the High Rank Urgent Quest "Rise to the Summit"
G Rank: Complete the G Rank Urgent Quest "Absolute Zero"
Fatalis: Complete the G Rank Special Quest "Legendary Black Dragon"
Crimson Fatalis: Complete the G Rank Special Quest "The End Times"
White Fatalis: Complete the G Rank Special Quest "Ancestral Dragon"

options.name: guild_depth
options.description:
What ranks to consider as checks. All ranks prior to the selected rank will be added.
Unranked is considered to be a part of Low Rank.

options.name: village_depth
options.description: What Village ranks to consider as checks. All ranks prior to the selected rank will be added.

options.name: training_quests
options.description: Enables checks for successfully clearing a Training quest.

options.name: treasure_quests
options.description: Enables checks for reaching specific thresholds within the Treasure quests provided by Treshi.

options.name: guild_card_awards
options.description:
Include Guild Card awards as checks. Only awards accessible to your available ranks will be checks.
Off: no Guild Card awards are checks
Exclude Grindy: excludes crowns, most farm awards, Guild Point,
On: all Guild Card awards are checks. This setting may be overrided with a host setting.

options.name: max_keys
options.description:
Maximum number of key quests to include in the pool of items.
This number may not be exact depending on other settings.

options.name: required_keys
options.description: Percentage of key quests required to unlock the final urgent quest.

options.name: filler_percentage
options.description: Percentage of non-required Key Quests to be converted to filler items (random weapons/armor/decorations).

options.name: trap_percentage
options.description: Percentage of filler items to be converted to trap items.

options.name: trap_weights
options.description: Relative weights for each individual trap item to appear when a trap item is created.

options.name: weapons
options.description:
Individual: Each individual weapon tree rank can be received separately, not in sequence.
Progressive: Each individual weapon tree rank is received in sequence from 1 to 10.
Consolidated: All weapon trees are consolidated into a single item for each rank.
Progressive Consolidated: All weapon trees are consolidated into a single item, received in sequence.

options.name: progressive_armor
options.description: When enabled, armor will be received progressively starting from rarity 1 to rarity 10.

options.name: quest_randomization
options.description: When enabled, quest targets and requirements will be randomized.

options.name: quest_difficulty_multiplier
options.description: Multiplier applied to large monster stats, lower being easier and higher being harder.

options.name: cash_only_equipment
options.description: When enabled, equipment no longer requires items for crafting/upgrading.

options.name: trap_link
options.description:
When enabled, traps you receive will be shared across compatible games, and you
will receive equivalent traps when other TrapLink players receive trap items.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.
